text.title: confidence-thresholded-cascade Decision Tree
text.summary: Decision tree from task description to cascade depth (none, 2-level, 3-level).

decision-tree.root-question: Is this task high-volume AND has elicitable confidence (classification, extraction, structured output)?

branch.conclusion: Skip cascade; use single strong model. Cost savings do not justify routing complexity.

branch.question: Is the task mission-critical (any error has high downstream cost)?

branch.conclusion: Use single strong model. Cascade is not appropriate when downside of cheap-leg errors exceeds compute savings.

branch.question: Does the difficulty distribution have a long tail (some tasks need deep reasoning, most are trivial)?

branch.conclusion: Two-level cascade is sufficient. Calibrate threshold on 100+ eval tasks; expect 60-80% cost savings.

branch.question: Does the p99 latency budget allow an extra 200-500ms hop?

branch.conclusion: Three-level cascade (Haiku → Sonnet → Opus). Calibrate both thresholds; expect 85-95% cost savings.
Two-level cascade only; accept lower savings to stay within latency SLA.

text.example: q-eligible → branch(yes) → q-critical → branch(no) → q-difficulty → branch(long_tail) → q-latency-budget → branch(yes) → conclusion(r4-cap-depth).
